X2.faithfulness_result: 10/11 confirmed faithful on first pass. split_digraph_strong_two_kernel_statement was found PROVABLY FALSE (a directed triangle with self-loops satisfied the hypotheses but had no strong 2-kernel); the object must be ORIENTED. All 11 now faithful and genuinely open.

axv_1509_06563_00.note: Encodes the bounded-clique generalisation: for nu>0 and k>=3, large chi and omega<k force holes of nu consecutive lengths.

axv_1509_06563_01.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.5 using the recovered context definition of k-constricting/constricting sets.

axv_1702_01094_00.note: Encodes the rainbow-hole question: bounded omega and large chi force, in every proper colouring, a hole with s consecutive rainbow vertices.

axv_1702_01094_01.note: Encodes the stable-cover question via a finite family of stable vertex subsets covering G and an s-vertex induced path uniquely covered at each path vertex.

axv_1705_04609_00.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.8: sufficiently large chi forces either a kappa-clique or holes of ell consecutive lengths.

axv_1910_00697_00.note: Encodes the solved Esperet problem as existence of an induced-hereditary chi-bounded graph class not bounded by any natural-coefficient polynomial in omega.

axv_2110_00278_00.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.3: for every forbidden forest H, H-induced-free graphs satisfy chi(G) <= omega(G)^c for some c>0.

axv_2201_08204_00.note: Encodes Question 3.1 as arbitrarily large chromatic graphs with omega=3 whose triangle-free induced subgraphs all have chromatic number at most 3.

erdos_1111.note: Encodes Erdos Problems #1111: bounded clique number plus large chi forces disjoint anticomplete vertex sets with large subset chromatic numbers.

std_galvin_r_dl_conjecture.note: Encodes the Galvin-Rodl conjecture: large chi and omega<=k force an induced subgraph with chi>=r and omega=2.

std_gy_rf_s_complementation_conjecture.note: Encodes the Gyarfas complementation conjecture: complements of classes whose induced subgraphs satisfy chi<=omega+c are chi-bounded.

std_gy_rf_s_conjecture_6_8_h_h_h_1_class.note: Encodes Gyarfas Conjecture 6.8: the class satisfying alpha(H) omega(H) >= |V(H)|-1 for every induced subgraph H is chi-bounded.

std_gy_rf_s_sumner_conjecture.note: Encodes the two-forbidden-graphs Gyarfas-Sumner statement using absolute bounded chromatic number, complete graphs, and forests.

X3.faithfulness_result: 13/13 confirmed faithful. No well-formed-but-unfaithful issues found in statements or local primitives. Residual assumptions recorded by reviewer: 'colouring' in the rainbow-hole row is proper colouring; natural-coefficient polynomials/natural exponents are faithful discretizations of polynomial bounds on integer graph invariants; finite indexed stable covers are no loss for finite graphs. The Scott-Seymour tournament out-neighbourhood row was explicitly deferred as a directed/tournament followup, not part of the undirected X3 batch. | Independent cross-check (distinct reader set): 13/13 faithful, quantifier-order trap cleared on every chi-bounded row.

erdos_551.note: Encodes the exact two-colour Ramsey formula R(C_k,K_n)=(k-1)(n-1)+1 for k>=n>=3, excluding n=k=3.

erdos_556.note: Encodes R_3(C_n) <= 4n-3 as the 3-colour complete-graph arrow at the stated bound, in the meaningful cycle regime n>=3.

erdos_582.note: Encodes the finite Folkman question: existence of a K4-free graph whose every 2-edge-colouring contains a monochromatic triangle.

erdos_617.note: Encodes the r-colouring statement for K_{r^2+1}: some r+1 vertex set has a missing colour on its induced edges.

erdos_639.note: Encodes the 2-colouring bound by counting unordered complete-graph edges that lie in no monochromatic triangle and cross-multiplying the n^2/4 inequality.

erdos_924.note: Encodes the general Folkman graph statement: for k>=2,l>=3 a K_{l+1}-free graph arrows K_l under every k-edge-colouring.

std_thomason_s_conjecture_on_book_ramsey.note: Encodes the diagonal book Ramsey bound r(B_n^(k)) <= 2^k(n+k-2)+2 using the standard k-spine, n-page book graph.

erdos_904.note: Encodes the Turan degree-sum result: if |E(G)| >= t_r(n), G has an r-clique whose degree sum satisfies n*sum d(x_i) >= 2r|E(G)|.

erdos_905.note: Encodes the n^2/4 edge-threshold book result by requiring an edge with at least n/6 common neighbours, cross-multiplied.

erdos_608.note: Encodes the C5-contained edge lower bound above the n^2/4 threshold, counting unordered edges lying on a 5-cycle and cross-multiplying 2n^2/9.

erdos_621.note: Encodes alpha_1(G)+tau_1(G)<=n^2/4 via finite edge-set maximum and triangle-hitting minimum predicates.

erdos_1010.note: Encodes the exact-edge supersaturation statement: if |E(G)|=floor(n^2/4)+t and t<floor(n/2), then G has at least t floor(n/2) triangles.

X10.faithfulness_result: Independent adversarial faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS, no fixes. Aharoni row: positive n,k guards, n-colour edge map, colour classes counted only on actual graph edges, and rainbow encoded as pairwise distinct colours on the cycle's consecutive edge sets. Smith row: r>=2, finite simple r-connected graph via k_connected, genuine longest cycles only, and vertex-intersection cardinality >= r.

axv_2201_09115_02.note: Encodes the fixed-s Woodall regime: for every s>=1, all sufficiently large t make every K_{s,t}-minor-free graph (s+t-1)-choosable.

axv_2512_17232_00.note: Encodes Conjecture 6: every graph and X,Y either has k pairwise vertex-disjoint anticomplete X-Y paths, or a set Z of size at most k-1 whose closed neighbourhood deletion leaves no X-Y path.

X11.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS. Woodall row preserves fixed-s then sufficiently-large-t quantifier order, K_{s,t}-minor exclusion, and choice-number bound s+t-1. Induced-Menger row preserves the either/or shape, pairwise anticomplete X-Y path packing, separator size <= k-1, and deletion of the closed neighbourhood N[Z].

std_woodall_s_list_colouring_conjecture_.note: Encodes Woodall's list-colouring conjecture: for all positive s,t, every K_{s,t}-minor-free graph has choice number at most s+t-1.

X12.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 1/1 PASS. Preserves the universal s,t quantifiers, K_{s,t}-minor exclusion, and list-chromatic/choice-number bound s+t-1, with positive guards for the complete bipartite parameters.

axv_1802_03727_01.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.4 with two positive unbounded functions x2,x3: every nonempty graph of minimum degree at least d contains either a clique of size at least x2(d) or a nonempty induced bipartite subgraph of minimum degree at least x3(d).

axv_1802_03727_03.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.6: for some positive thresholds d0,g0, every nonempty graph of girth at least g0 and minimum degree at least d0 contains a nonempty induced bipartite subgraph of minimum degree at least 3.

X13.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS after fixing the nonempty-witness guard. Conjecture 1.4 keeps positive functions tending to infinity and the clique-or-nonempty-induced-bipartite-subgraph dichotomy. Conjecture 1.6 keeps existential d0,g0 thresholds, girth/min-degree hypotheses, and the nonempty induced bipartite subgraph min-degree-three conclusion.

std_biedl_demaine_duncan_fleischer_kobou.note: Encodes the subcubic matching conjecture as the existence of a matching M with 9|M| >= 3n+n2, where n2 counts degree-two vertices.

std_andersen_s_conjecture.note: Encodes Andersen's conjecture: every proper edge-colouring of K_n has a rainbow path with n-2 edges, represented by a vertex sequence of size n-1.

X14.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS. The matching row preserves subcubic graphs and the 9nu >= 3n+n2 inequality via an explicit matching witness. The Andersen row preserves proper edge-colouring of K_n and a rainbow path of length n-2.

axv_1611_03196_02.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.14: every partition of a graph's edge set admits a matching meeting each part in at least floor(|E_i|/(Delta(H)+2)) edges.

axv_1611_03196_03.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.15: for every number of edge-sets there is c(m) so every positive-maximum-degree bipartite graph has a matching of size at least |E(G)|/Delta(G)-c(m) while meeting each set in at most ceil(|E_i|/Delta(G)) edges.

X15.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS. Conjecture 1.14 preserves the edge-set partition hypothesis, matching witness, and floor(|E_i|/(Delta+2)) lower bounds. Conjecture 1.15 preserves the forall-m exists-c quantifier order, bipartite host, arbitrary edge subsets, matching lower bound up to c(m), and ceiling upper bounds; a positive-Delta guard avoids the source fraction's undefined Delta=0 case.

std_small_quasi_kernel_conjecture_erd_s_.note: Encodes the Erdős-Székely small quasi-kernel conjecture: every loopless digraph with no source has a 2-kernel K with 2|K| <= |G|.

std_spiro_s_quasi_kernel_conjecture.note: Encodes Spiro's conjecture: every loopless digraph has a 2-kernel K such that at least half the vertices lie in K or have an in-neighbour in K.

X16.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS. Both rows use the X2 2-kernel notion: arc-stable and every vertex 2-covered from K. The small quasi-kernel row keeps the no-source hypothesis and half-size bound. The Spiro row keeps the half one-covered conclusion. Loopless guards restore the conventional digraph semantics because Rocq diGraphType permits loops.

std_stein_s_conjecture_on_oriented_paths.note: Encodes Stein's conjecture: every nonempty oriented graph with strict minimum semidegree greater than k/2 contains every orientation of the k-edge path.

std_sumner_s_conjecture.note: Encodes Sumner's conjecture: every oriented tree of order n>1 is (2n-2)-unavoidable in tournaments.

X17.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS. Stein's row uses strict k < 2*delta^0, not the threshold >= variant from X2, and quantifies over every oriented k-edge path. Sumner's row uses the existing tournament-unavoidability predicate and preserves the order n>1 and 2n-2 bound.

axv_1611_03196_00.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.6: every partition of a finite path's vertices has an independent set S with per-part lower bounds |S cap V_i| >= |V_i|/2 - b_i, total sum b_i <= m/2, and each b_i <= 1, using cross-multiplied natural inequalities.

axv_1611_03196_01.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.9: every edge partition of K_{n,n} and distinguished part j admits a perfect matching meeting all other parts at least floor(|E_i|/n) times and part j at least floor(|E_j|/n)-1 times.

std_brualdi_stein_conjecture.note: Encodes the Brualdi-Stein special case: if E(K_{n,n}) is partitioned into n parts of size n, there is a matching of size at least n-1 using at most one edge from each part.

X18.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 3/3 PASS. Conjecture 1.6 preserves the path host, vertex partition, independent-set witness, per-part half-minus-b_i lower bounds, total b_i <= m/2, and b_i <= 1 via integer cross-multiplication. Conjecture 1.9 preserves K_{n,n}, an edge partition, the distinguished part j, a perfect matching witness, and the floor lower bounds. Brualdi-Stein preserves the size-n parts and 'all but possibly one' transversal as a size >= n-1 matching with at most one chosen edge from each part.

std_behzad_chartrand_wall_conjecture_gir.note: Encodes the Behzad-Chartrand-Wall conjecture: every nonempty loopless r-regular digraph of directed girth at least g has at least r(g-1)+1 vertices.

std_lichiardopol_s_conjecture.note: Encodes Lichiardopol's conjecture: for every k there is a threshold g(k) so every nonempty loopless digraph with minimum outdegree at least g(k) contains k vertex-disjoint directed cycles of pairwise distinct lengths.

X19.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS. The regular-girth row preserves r-regularity, directed girth at least g, and the r(g-1)+1 order bound; nonempty and loopless guards match conventional digraph semantics and avoid vacuous empty/loop artefacts in Rocq diGraphType. The Lichiardopol row preserves forall-k exists-g(k), pointwise minimum outdegree, vertex-disjoint directed cycles, and pairwise distinct cycle lengths, with the same conventional loopless/nonempty guards.

std_burning_number_conjecture.note: Encodes the burning number conjecture: every nonempty connected graph has a burning sequence of length ceil(sqrt(|V(G)|)), represented by radius t-1-i closed neighbourhood growth from the i-th chosen vertex.

std_gy_rf_s_s_rk_zy_monochromatic_compon.note: Encodes the Gyarfas-Sarkozy conjecture: for k>=3, minimum degree at least (1-(k-1)/k^2)n in a k-edge-coloured graph forces a monochromatic connected vertex set of order at least n/(k-1), with the degree and size inequalities cross-multiplied over naturals.

X20.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS. The burning row preserves connected finite graphs, b(G) <= ceil(sqrt n) as existence of a length-ceil-sqrt burning cover, and the ceil-sqrt relation is minimal. The monochromatic-component row preserves k>=3, arbitrary edge colouring, the exact minimum-degree threshold via k^2*delta >= (k^2-(k-1))*n, and the n/(k-1) component lower bound as existence of a monochromatically connected vertex set S with n <= (k-1)|S|.

std_kelly_manvel_conjecture_reconstructi.note: Encodes the Kelly-Manvel row: for every r there is N such that every n-vertex graph with N<=n is reconstructible from its (n-r)-deck.

std_n_dl_s_conjecture_deck_reconstructio.note: Encodes the Nydl tree deck row: for n>=4 and ell>=floor(n/2)+1, two n-vertex trees with the same ell-deck are isomorphic.

std_spinoza_west_connectedness_threshold.note: Encodes the Spinoza-West connectedness threshold row: for n>=6 and ell>=floor(n/2)+1, same ell-deck implies connectedness is equivalent.

X21.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 3/3 PASS. All three rows use an ell-deck as a bijection between ell-vertex induced cards up to isomorphism. Kelly-Manvel preserves forall-r exists-N and the (n-r)-deck with an N<=n guard. Nydl preserves n>=4, ell>=floor(n/2)+1, trees, same ell-deck, and graph isomorphism. Spinoza-West preserves n>=6, ell>=floor(n/2)+1, same ell-deck, and connectedness equivalence; this row uses statement_text because source_text is null in the manifest.

std_jaeger_s_conjecture_high_girth_plana.note: Encodes Jaeger's conjecture: every planar graph of girth at least 4k has a homomorphism to C_(2k+1).

X22.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 1/1 PASS. Preserves the planar hypothesis, girth at least 4k, target odd cycle C_(2k+1), and graph homomorphism conclusion; the circular-colouring paraphrase is not separately encoded because it is stated as an equivalent explanation in the source text.

std_alon_grytczuk_ha_uszczak_riordan_con.note: Encodes bounded nonrepetitive chromatic number for planar graphs as an existential global colour bound k.

std_planar_linear_arboricity_conjecture.note: Encodes the planar linear arboricity conjecture: maximum degree Delta>=5 implies la(G)=ceil(Delta/2).

X23.faithfulness_result: Faithfulness readback: 2/2 PASS. The nonrepetitive-colouring row preserves the exists-k forall-planar-G boundedness shape and models nonrepetitive paths by excluding equal adjacent colour blocks. The linear-arboricity row preserves planar graphs, maximum degree at least 5, and equality with ceil(Delta/2) via a minimum edge-colouring into colour classes that are linear forests.

std_akbari_etesami_mahini_mahmoody_quest.note: Encodes the Akbari-Etesami-Mahini-Mahmoody question: every 1-factorization of K_n has a rainbow cycle of length at least n-2.

std_kotzig_s_perfect_1_factorization_con.note: Encodes Kotzig's conjecture: K_n has a perfect 1-factorization for every even n>2.

X71.faithfulness_result: CORRECTED 2026-07-13. The prior '1/1 PASS via the dichromatic_bounded wrapper' was WRONG: the source (1704.07219, 2212.02272) says Forb_ind(F) is chi-vec-BOUNDED (dichromatic number <= f(omega-vec)), but the file used dichromatic_bounded = a CONSTANT bound. The constant form is FALSE: Forb_ind(F) contains every tournament (a tournament has no induced copy of any oriented forest on >=3 vertices), and tournaments have unbounded dichromatic number, so no uniform B exists. Rewritten to the faithful function form 'exists f, forall oriented F-free D, dicolorableb D (f (omega(underlying D)))' -- the corpus directed-chi-boundedness idiom (identical to X54); omega(underlying) = largest sub-tournament = omega-vec. Not violated by tournamnents (omega-vec large there); consistent with X123 (forbidding a transitive tournament bounds omega-vec => chi-vec-finite). Compiles axiom-free; genuinely open.
